workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just came out of whitespace analysis with Northwind Traders. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 12:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 09:11 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Northwind Traders whitespace analysis

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 14:49 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Northwind Traders: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-J trap (Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-J failure mode (Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-J -- Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread
